Just outside town, Birchtown stands as the largest Black Loyalist settlement in the province — a nationally significant chapter of the same 1783 story. The economy still runs on the water. Lobster fishing and boatbuilding anchor Shelburne, carrying on the dory-building craft the town is known for, while tourism and forestry round out the local base.
